title: CUDA kernel for ggml_dsv4_rope_tail (V4 CUDA port Stream B1)

history.note: Plan + spec pre-written via brainstorming + writing-plans skills. Stream A merged; Stream B1 is now unblocked. Plan pre-fixed with Stream A lessons learned (-b CPU,CUDA flag + count assertion). Starting at plan-review for codex independent review on the pre-fixed plan.

REVISE: Pre-applied fixes (-b CPU,CUDA + COUNT>=5) confirmed correct, but 3 technical blockers: (1) op_params layout in plan Step 4.1 swaps inverse and floats vs Metal dispatch at ggml-metal-ops.cpp:1606-1623 — inverse is at slot 3, floats at slots 4-9; (2) plan implements NORMAL pair rotation only but Stream A registers a NEOX edge case (test-backend-ops.cpp:8836-8838) with separate is_neox branch at metal:4943-4967; (3) YaRN helper omits corr_dims/n_ctx_orig/beta_fast/beta_slow — needs rope_yarn pattern from rope.cu:22-41 with ggml_rope_yarn_corr_dims. Workflow note (AGENTS.md AI-commit) does not apply at internal-merge stage.

Plan revisions applied: (1) Step 4.1 dispatch reads i32[0..3] + f32[4..9] via memcpy matching Metal verbatim; (2) Step 5.2 kernel body has both is_neox and NORMAL branches, line-for-line port of metal:4943-4990; (3) Step 4.1 helper rewritten as dsv4_rope_yarn<bool forward>(...) with rope_corr_dims struct, matching rope.cu:22-41; ggml_rope_yarn_corr_dims called host-side. Step 6.3 supports_op tightened with mode filter. Step 8.2 push target origin→mine.

APPROVE: 'The revised plan now matches the Metal dispatch and kernel semantics for dsv4_rope_tail: op_params layout, launch shape, NEOX vs NORMAL rotation, inverse handling, freq_factors indexing, and YaRN corr_dims all line up with the local references. The CUDA registration/supports_op plan is scoped correctly, the test gate uses -b CPU,CUDA with a count assertion, and the expected diff stays under ggml/src/ggml-cuda/.'

Implemented per approved plan in worktree /Users/cchuter/work/llama.cpp.B-rope-tail. New files: dsv4-rope-tail.{cuh,cu}. Modified: ggml-cuda.cu (include, dispatcher case, supports_op case with mode∈{NORMAL,NEOX} + F32 + contiguity). CMakeLists.txt untouched (GLOBs *.cu/*.cuh). Commit 48da849ce.

APPROVE: 'The CUDA implementation matches the Metal op_params layout, launch geometry, byte-stride pointer arithmetic, NORMAL and NEOX tail-rotation paths, optional freq_factors indexing, inverse sin flip, and YaRN math/corr_dims ordering from the existing CUDA RoPE helper. Dispatcher registration and supports_op are present with the required mode filter, F32/contiguity constraints, and CUDA_CHECK(cudaGetLastError()) after launch. Diff scope is limited to ggml/src/ggml-cuda/; CMake does not need an edit because the CUDA sources are globbed, and I do not see dead code, TODOs, debug prints, or scratch comments.'

BLOCKED: CUDA gate cannot run from darwin/arm64 orchestrator (no nvcc). Required on RTX 6000 Ada / 5090 host: cmake -B build-cuda -DGGML_CUDA=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES="89;120" && cmake --build build-cuda -j --target test-backend-ops && ./build-cuda/bin/test-backend-ops -b CPU,CUDA -o DSV4_ROPE_TAIL 2>&1 | tee /tmp/v4-cuda-B-rope-tail-test.log | tail -30 && COUNT=$(grep -E "^\s+[0-9]+/[0-9]+ tests passed" /tmp/v4-cuda-B-rope-tail-test.log | tail -1 | grep -oE "^\s+[0-9]+" | tr -d ' ') && test "${COUNT:-0}" -ge 5. Implementation pushed to remote `mine` for preservation; transition to done/ and fast-forward into feat/v4-port-cuda awaits gate pass on CUDA host. Follows same pattern as v4-port-cuda-B-expand.
5/5 DSV4_ROPE_TAIL passed on RTX 5090 (CC 12.0) via CUDA 12.8 + SM_120 native. Fast-forwarded into feat/v4-port-cuda and pushed.
